brick: the KV-CACHED CAUSAL GQA DECODE STEP runs on the M4 Max GPU — real llama3.2 autoregressive generation on silicon (n_q query heads sharing n_kv<n_q KV heads), the cache bit-identical to the full causal GQA recompute
author: Sema (Opus 4.8, autonomous sema-native-ml-walk)
north_star_rung: GPU driver-replacement / real-llama-on-GPU: the named next stone from 3zj (kv-gqa-llama-block.fk, lgqa-generate-causal four-way 255, #3601) — 'emit this cached GQA decode step to Metal (mirroring jte-gqa-llama-block-fwd-causal-msl #3590) + run on the M4 GPU with an fp32 CPU-mirror bit-exact gate (the 3y/#3412 lane for GQA)'. 3zj proved the GQA cached decode step four-way at the RECIPE altitude; this is its GPU realization. The single-head/MHA decode kernel (jte-llama-block-decode-step-msl #3412) becomes the n_q=n_kv=1 special case of a kernel that runs the real 24->8 grouping at decode time, over a narrower n_kv*HD-wide cache.
lane: NEW-GPU-kernel + persistent-cache loop + bit-exact gate (the 3m/3s/3u/3y/3zh/3zi lane). Design (the GQA decode ABI — q to dq=n_q*HD, k/v to the SMALLER KVD=n_kv*HD written to cache slot p; the per-query-head grouped attention over the grown prefix with kvh=h/group; the RoPE width KVD for the new key; threading one persistent KVD-wide cache across S dispatches; fold-order parity so cache==recompute) was the cost; both proofs were seconds because the body had ALREADY built the single-head GPU decode template (jte-llama-block-decode-step-msl #3412), the full causal GQA block GPU kernel as the recompute baseline (jte-gqa-llama-block-fwd-causal-msl #3590), AND the proven four-way GQA cached-decode recipe (kv-gqa-llama-block.fk, lgqa-generate-causal four-way 255 #3601).
read_the_body: The two GPU templates were both already standing: jte-llama-block-decode-step-msl (#3412, the MHA decode step over a persistent cache) and jte-gqa-llama-block-fwd-causal-msl (#3590, the whole GQA block forward, the recompute baseline). The brick was their FUSION — the MHA decode-step structure with GQA grouping. RMSNorm1/RMSNorm2 + the SwiGLU FFN are jte-llama-dec-rms1/rms2/ffn VERBATIM (those reference only oN1/oH/oN2/oG/oU/oACT, never the attention offsets), so only the projection widths (k/v to KVD), the RoPE width, and the per-query-head grouped attention loop changed. The 19th time read-the-body paid off — no recipe re-derivation, the proven fp64 recipe (#3601) IS the parity oracle.
what_changed: form/form-stdlib/jit-tensor-emit.fk: added jte-gqa-llama-block-decode-step-msl (+ jte-gqa-dec-sig/off/proj/rope/attn/out) — emits lgqa-generate-causal's per-step decode as ONE Metal kernel over a PERSISTENT KV cache. Each step p: RMSNorm1(x_p) -> project q to dq=n_q*HD and k/v to the SMALLER KVD=n_kv*HD from the single new token -> RoPE q/k by p -> APPEND the RoPE'd k_p/v_p into cache slot p (KVD wide) -> per query head h, attend its HD-wide q-slice over the grown prefix cache[0..p] of KV head (h/group) -> Wo/residual/RMSNorm2/SwiGLU/residual -> y_p. RMSNorm1/RMSNorm2 + SwiGLU FFN are jte-llama-dec-rms1/rms2/ffn VERBATIM. group=1 (n_kv=n_q) IS the single-head decode per head (core-abstraction-first, no parallel path). RoPE base-10000 = cfg10 special case.

form/form-stdlib/tests/gqa-llama-block-decode-emit-band.fk: new emit band — the full emitted f32 MSL for form_gqa_llama_decode_step_f32 equals the canonical 6650-byte text (the cross-kernel byte-identity anchor), emission is a function of the name, deterministic. Generated by splitting the canonical bytes on the kernel name (no hand-transcription). Verdict 7.

scripts/metal_gqa_llama_block_decode_audit.sh: the on-hardware witness — emits BOTH the GQA decode step AND the full causal GQA recompute block, compiles mathMode-safe (IEEE, no fast-math contraction), runs both on the M4 Max GPU over the GQA grouping fixture extended to S=3 (the inductive step), gates the GQA decode loop (S dispatches over one persistent KVD-wide cache) == the full causal GQA recompute (one dispatch) BIT-FOR-BIT, AND the decode loop tracks the fp64 recipe within fp32 epsilon.
scripts/INDEX.md: regenerated to list the new audit script.

emit_band_four_way.result: 1 ok, 0 divergent — Go/Rust/TS/fkwu emit the 6650-byte GQA decode-step MSL byte-identical; fourth arm crossed

recipe_four_way.pr: #3601 — proven four-way at the recipe altitude, BIT-IDENTICAL to lgqa-block-causal (#3590, four-way 15)
recipe_four_way.note: this GPU brick is the silicon realization of that proven recipe; the recompute baseline kernel (jte-gqa-llama-block-fwd-causal-msl) is itself GPU-proven (#3590)

on_gpu.decode_kernel: form_gqa_llama_decode_step_f32 (6650 bytes, every byte authored by jte-gqa-llama-block-decode-step-msl)
on_gpu.fixture: kv-gqa-llama-block-band / llama-gqa-block-causal-band claim 8 extended to S=3 (the inductive step): d=4, n_q=2 query heads, n_kv=1 KV head, HD=2, scale 1/sqrt(2), RoPE base-10000 (cfg10) — both query heads 0,1 -> kv0 (the h/group mapping llama3.2 runs at 24 -> 8), narrower KVD=2-wide cache
on_gpu.gate_1_equivalence: GQA decode loop (3 dispatches over one persistent KVD-wide cache) == full causal GQA recompute (one dispatch) BIT-FOR-BIT on silicon (every bitPattern identical) — the GQA KV cache reproduces the recompute exactly because a cached k_i/v_i depends only on position i and the fold order is identical
on_gpu.gate_2_recipe: GPU fp32 GQA decode tracks fp64 lgqa-block-causal *1e6 within fp32 epsilon (max|Δ| = 0.5)
on_gpu.token0_pin: y[0][0]*1e6 = 2147164 matches llama-gqa-block-causal-band's published claim-8 pin (the GQA recompute baseline), and the cached decode reproduces it bit-for-bit

why_bit_identical: A cached k_i/v_i = RoPE(Wk·RMSNorm1(x_i), i) / Wv·RMSNorm1(x_i) is narrower (KVD = n_kv*HD wide) but still depends ONLY on position i, never the future. So the grown cache at step p is exactly the prefix [k_0..k_p] the full causal GQA kernel's mask (while t < s+1u) cuts at query p, walked in IDENTICAL fold order; per query head, the decode kernel dots q's HD-slice against KV head (h/group)'s cached prefix — the same arguments the recompute kernel folds. Incremental cached GQA decode reproduces the whole-sequence recompute exactly on silicon. The S=3 loop proves the cache threads correctly past the boundary through the grouping (the autoregressive invariant: appending a future token never changes an earlier token's output).
carriers: form-kernel-go (the mouth that emits the MSL), swiftc + Metal.framework (the driver-organ idiom — allowed host carriers per host-kernel.form host-resource-access). The single-thread (gid==0) serial walk matches the recipe bit-exactly; parallelizing across query heads/tokens is the named follow-up. The transcendentals stay the recipe's OWN (Newton sqrt, fexp Taylor, RoPE's fsin/fcos/fln Taylor), never Metal's.
estimate_vs_actual: est ~1h, actual ~40m — NEW-GPU-kernel lane, matches 3m/3s/3u/3y/3zh/3zi. Design (the GQA decode ABI, the narrower cache, the per-head grouped attention over the prefix) was the cost; the four-way emit-band proof was seconds and both on-GPU gates passed first try because the body had built both GPU templates (#3412, #3590) and the proven four-way recipe (#3601).
named_next_stones: the multi-layer GQA decode stack on the GPU (the 3za lane for GQA) — stack this decode kernel across the real 28 llama3.2 blocks, each layer its own persistent device cache buffer
run the GQA decode step over REAL llama3.2:3b GGUF weights at full width (n_q=24, n_kv=8, HD=128, d=3072) — the load path q4k/q6k/f16 is already Form-native four-way
wire the full generation loop (tokenize-llama #3573 -> multi-layer GQA decode -> greedy argmax #3402 over real llama3.2:3b GGUF) — the first end-to-end native llama token
parallelize across query heads/tokens (one threadgroup per head)
